author: codex
scope: validator/rule lane; artifact-only prep; no generated-output commands; no provider-backed post-ingest commands

recommendation.summary: Allow event records to carry source-backed claims via has_claim. The remaining eight rows are meeting events linked to claims discussed, reviewed, approved, delayed, or described during that meeting event. This is a narrow endpoint-shape gap analogous to the already accepted event-carrier shapes, and does not require widening unrelated relation kinds.
why_not_data_cleanup: All eight subjects are canonical event records, and all eight objects are canonical claim records.
The relation kind is semantically direct: the meeting event has, reviews, discusses, or records the claim.
Four rows are already conductor-produced batch013 replacements for earlier malformed timeline relations, with descriptions explicitly preserving the event and claim endpoints as has_claim.
Replacing these rows again would add churn without correcting endpoint identity or relation direction.

exact_patch_plan.relations_ts_change: In RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES.has_claim.subject, add "event" to the existing subject list. Keep the object list as ["claim"].
exact_patch_plan.relations_test_change: Add expect(relationEndpointShapeIssue("has_claim", "event", "claim")).toBeUndefined(); to the relation helper accepted-shapes test. Add or update one negative assertion for has_claim so the expected-subject message includes event if the test asserts the full expected-subject string.
non_goals: Do not widen has_metric claim -> metric_claim in this patch.
Do not widen has_timeline_event claim, treatment_component, or metric_claim subjects in this patch.
Do not change generated canonical/materialized outputs.
Do not run materialize, export-jsonl, full validate, provider/profile, or other post-ingest generated-output commands.

inventory_context.method: Targeted bun --eval over readCanonicalRecords() plus relationEndpointShapeIssue(); not full validate.

pending_retirement_exclusions.reason: Batches 019, 020, and 021 are durable conductor inputs pending the next serialized materialize/export/validate checkpoint. Their retired submission ids were excluded where applicable to avoid duplicating already-queued data cleanup.

top_buckets_after_exclusions.classification: defer; plausible next rule-gap review, but not bundled into this narrow has_claim patch
defer/data-likely; treatment components are not implemented projects under current rule
defer; source metric ownership needs separate evidence review
defer/data-likely based on batch020 precedent for claim timeline context

object.display_name: MTA Code of Ethics Originally Adopted 2005

candidate_rows.evidence_text: 4. REVIEW AND APPROVE MTA CODES OF ETHICS / All-Agency Code of Ethics / Originally Adopted May 25, 2005
candidate_rows.reason: The committee meeting event reviewed/approved a code-of-ethics claim.

object.display_name: Mission Statement Report Submitted as Part of MTA 2023 Annual Report

candidate_rows.evidence_text: 5. REVIEW AND APPROVE MISSION STATEMENT AND PERFORMANCE MEASURE REPORT / Metropolitan Transportation Authority Mission Statement, Measurements, and Performance Indicators Report Covering Fiscal Year 2023
candidate_rows.reason: The meeting event carries a source-backed mission/performance report claim.

object.display_name: No proposed revisions to Procurement Guidelines

candidate_rows.evidence_text: 3. REVIEW AND APPROVE PROCUREMENT GUIDELINES / Staff Summary Procurement Guidelines - Page 5 / It is recommended that the MTA Board approve the annexed All Agency Procurement Guidelines and All Agency Guidelines for Procurement of Services.
candidate_rows.reason: The meeting event reviews/approves a procurement-guidelines claim.

candidate_rows.evidence_text: 6. REVIEW MTA WHISTLEBLOWER POLICY / WHISTLEBLOWER PROTECTION / To encourage and enable the reporting by MTA employees of wrongful acts, protect MTA employees from retaliation for whistleblowing and ensure that the MTA complies with applicable law protecting whistleblowers.
candidate_rows.reason: The meeting event carries a reviewed whistleblower-policy claim.

candidate_rows.evidence_text: Board Member Albert asked about the status of bus operator enclosures and whether a pilot program has begun on any routes. Mr. Warren stated that the enclosures have been designed and installed in several busses. / improvements have been made to the enclosures. Robert Diehl spoke briefly about the full enclosure that is still being evaluated but will be coming soon.
candidate_rows.reason: The safety committee meeting event records a bus-operator-enclosure status claim.

candidate_rows.evidence_text: Chair Lieber asked if there are any changes to the Work Plan. Patrick Warren stated there was a change to the plan. Fare Evasion was originally going to be briefed but will be done next month in a different venue.
candidate_rows.reason: The meeting event records a claim about the fare-evasion briefing being deferred.

candidate_rows.evidence_text: Chair Lieber stated there was no quorum, so approval of the minutes from the July 2023 Safety Committee would be delayed until a time a quorum is present.
candidate_rows.reason: The meeting event records a procedural claim about minutes approval.

candidate_rows.evidence_text: Chair Lieber pointed out we are in control of policing with MTAPD on the commuter railroads and partner with NYPD in the subways, and work to deter employee assaults.
candidate_rows.reason: The meeting event records a source-backed claim about policing arrangements.

deferred_neighbor_bucket.why_deferred: This bucket may also be a validator rule gap because claims can carry metrics, but it has different semantics from event-owned claims and should receive its own evidence-backed prep rather than being bundled into the has_claim event-subject patch.

commands_run.outcome: Confirmed has_metric event -> metric_claim, has_timeline_event source -> event, and has_timeline_event event -> event are present; has_claim still omits event subjects.
Confirmed tests cover the three applied validator patches and do not cover has_claim event -> claim.

commands_run.command: rg -n "relation-rule-or-data-next2|has_claim event|has_metric claim|has_timeline_event event|batch020|batch019" LOG.md data/review_notes packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts packages/pipeline/test/records/relations.test.ts -g '*.md' -g '*.json' -g '*.jsonl' -g '*.ts'
commands_run.outcome: Found current rule/data context, including pending relation batches and the previous event-timeline prep.
Confirmed relation batches019,020,021 are durable conductor inputs pending materialize, and that the three validator patches named in this task are applied.

commands_run.command: bun --eval 'import { readCanonicalRecords } from "./packages/pipeline/src/materialize/materialize.ts"; import { relationEndpointShapeIssue } from "./packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts"; ... targeted endpoint-shape inventory excluding batches019-021 retire ids ...'
commands_run.outcome: Targeted inventory only: 102 unexpected endpoint-shape rows after exclusions. Top buckets were has_claim event->claim count 8 and has_metric claim->metric_claim count 8.

commands_run.command: bun --eval 'import { readCanonicalRecords } from "./packages/pipeline/src/materialize/materialize.ts"; import { relationEndpointShapeIssue } from "./packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts"; ... print has_claim event->claim and has_metric claim->metric_claim rows ...'
commands_run.outcome: Printed all rows in the top two buckets, including record ids, submission ids, endpoint ids/kinds, display names, and evidence refs.
